# ============================================================
#  Rent & Roster: Student Housing Affordability EDA
#  Author: Elma M.
#  Data: ACS 1-Year Estimates via tidycensus API
#        Variables: B19049 (income by age), B25064 (median rent)
# ============================================================


```{r}
# LOAD LIBRARIES
library(readr)        # reading CSV files
library(dplyr)        # data wrangling
library(tidyr)        # reshaping data
library(purrr)        # looping with map_dfr
library(janitor)      # clean_names, tabyl
library(DataExplorer) # introduce(), plot_missing()
library(tidycensus)   # pulling Census ACS data
library(ggplot2)      # plotting
library(ggrepel)      # non-overlapping labels on scatter plot
library(forcats)      # reordering factors in plots
library(scales)       # formatting axis labels
 
# Load my Census API key (saved from earlier setup)
readRenviron("~/.Renviron")
 
# Create output folder for saved figures
dir.create("figures", showWarnings = FALSE)
```

```{r}
# LOAD & CLEAN HOUSEHOLD INCOME DATA
# Source: Census Bureau Historical Income Table H-8
# This gives us median household income by state, 1984-2024
 
raw_data <- read_csv(
  "../data/raw/Median Household Income by State 1984 to 2024.csv",
  skip = 7,
  col_names = FALSE,
  show_col_types = FALSE
)
 
# Extract year and column type rows
year_row <- as.character(unlist(raw_data[1, ]))
type_row <- as.character(unlist(raw_data[2, ]))
 
# Keep only "Median income" columns + State column
state_col   <- 1
median_cols <- which(type_row == "Median income")
 
Household_median <- raw_data[-c(1, 2), c(state_col, median_cols)]
 
# Rename columns to year labels
income_years <- year_row[median_cols]
colnames(Household_median) <- c("State", income_years)
 
# Convert income columns to numeric
Household_median <- Household_median %>%
  mutate(across(-State, ~ as.numeric(gsub(",", "", .))))
 
# Clean up state names and rows
Household_median$State[1] <- "United States (Median average)"
Household_median <- Household_median[1:52, ]
Household_median <- Household_median %>%
  select(-matches("\\((39|40)\\)$"))
colnames(Household_median) <- gsub("\\s*\\(.*\\)", "", colnames(Household_median))
 
cat("Income data loaded:", nrow(Household_median), "states,",
    ncol(Household_median) - 1, "years\n")
```
```{r}
# PULL ACS RENT + AGE-SPECIFIC INCOME DATA
# Source: American Community Survey 1-Year Estimates
# B19049 = Median household income by age of householder
# B25064 = Median gross rent
# Note: 2020 ACS 1-year was NOT released due to COVID
 
years <- c(2015, 2016, 2017, 2018, 2019, 2021, 2022, 2023)
 
cat("Pulling ACS income by age data (this takes ~30 seconds)...\n")
income_by_age_raw <- map_dfr(years, function(yr) {
  get_acs(
    geography = "state",
    variables = c(
      "under25"  = "B19049_002",  # householders under 25
      "age25_44" = "B19049_003"   # householders 25-44 (comparison)
    ),
    year   = yr,
    survey = "acs1"
  ) %>%
    mutate(year = yr)
})
 
cat("Pulling ACS median gross rent data...\n")
rent_raw <- map_dfr(years, function(yr) {
  get_acs(
    geography = "state",
    variables = "B25064_001",
    year   = yr,
    survey = "acs1"
  ) %>%
    mutate(year = yr)
})
 
cat("ACS data pulled successfully!\n")
```
```{r}
# BUILD 2023 BURDEN DATASET
# Rent burden = annual rent / annual income * 100
# Standard thresholds:
#   < 30% = affordable
#   30-50% = burdened
#   > 50% = severely burdened
 
# 2023 income by age group
income_2023 <- income_by_age_raw %>%
  filter(year == 2023) %>%
  select(State = NAME, variable, estimate) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = variable, values_from = estimate) %>%
  rename(
    income_under25 = under25,
    income_25_44   = age25_44
  )
 
# 2023 annual rent (monthly * 12)
rent_2023 <- rent_raw %>%
  filter(year == 2023) %>%
  select(State = NAME, median_rent_monthly = estimate) %>%
  mutate(median_rent_annual = median_rent_monthly * 12)
 
# Merge and calculate burden ratios
burden_2023 <- income_2023 %>%
  inner_join(rent_2023, by = "State") %>%
  filter(State != "Puerto Rico") %>%
  mutate(
    # Under-25 burden
    burden_under25 = (median_rent_annual / income_under25) * 100,
    burden_cat_under25 = case_when(
      burden_under25 < 30  ~ "Affordable (<30%)",
      burden_under25 < 50  ~ "Burdened (30-50%)",
      burden_under25 >= 50 ~ "Severely Burdened (>50%)"
    ),
    burden_cat_under25 = factor(burden_cat_under25,
      levels = c("Affordable (<30%)", "Burdened (30-50%)",
                 "Severely Burdened (>50%)")),
 
    # 25-44 burden (comparison group)
    burden_25_44 = (median_rent_annual / income_25_44) * 100,
    burden_cat_25_44 = case_when(
      burden_25_44 < 30  ~ "Affordable (<30%)",
      burden_25_44 < 50  ~ "Burdened (30-50%)",
      burden_25_44 >= 50 ~ "Severely Burdened (>50%)"
    ),
    burden_cat_25_44 = factor(burden_cat_25_44,
      levels = c("Affordable (<30%)", "Burdened (30-50%)",
                 "Severely Burdened (>50%)"))
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(burden_under25))
 
cat("Burden dataset built:", nrow(burden_2023), "states\n\n")
```

```{r}
# QUICK DATA OVERVIEW
cat("=== DATASET OVERVIEW ===\n")
introduce(burden_2023)
plot_missing(burden_2023)
 
cat("\n=== SUMMARY STATISTICS ===\n")
burden_2023 %>%
  summarise(
    mean_burden_under25   = round(mean(burden_under25, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    median_burden_under25 = round(median(burden_under25, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    min_burden_under25    = round(min(burden_under25, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    max_burden_under25    = round(max(burden_under25, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    mean_burden_25_44     = round(mean(burden_25_44, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    median_burden_25_44   = round(median(burden_25_44, na.rm = TRUE), 1)
  ) %>%
  print()
 
cat("\n=== BURDEN CATEGORY COUNTS (Under 25) ===\n")
burden_2023 %>% count(burden_cat_under25) %>% print()
 
cat("\n=== BURDEN CATEGORY COUNTS (Ages 25-44) ===\n")
burden_2023 %>% count(burden_cat_25_44) %>% print()
 
```
```{r}
# DEFINE SHARED THEME AND COLORS
 
COLORS <- c(
  "Affordable (<30%)"        = "#27ae60",
  "Burdened (30-50%)"        = "#e67e22",
  "Severely Burdened (>50%)" = "#c0392b"
)
 
theme_rentburden <- function() {
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
    theme(
      plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16,
                                      color = "#1a1a2e",
                                      margin = margin(b = 6)),
      plot.subtitle    = element_text(size = 11, color = "#555577",
                                      margin = margin(b = 14)),
      plot.caption     = element_text(size = 9, color = "#888899",
                                      hjust = 0, margin = margin(t = 10)),
      axis.title       = element_text(size = 11, color = "#333355"),
      axis.text        = element_text(size = 10, color = "#444466"),
      panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "#e8e8f0", linewidth = 0.5),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      legend.position  = "top",
      legend.title     = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10),
      legend.text      = element_text(size = 10),
      plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "#fafafa", color = NA),
      panel.background = element_rect(fill = "#fafafa", color = NA),
      plot.margin      = margin(16, 20, 12, 16)
    )
}
```
```{r}
# PLOT 1: DISTRIBUTION OF UNDER-25 BURDEN RATIOS
p1 <- ggplot(burden_2023,
             aes(x = burden_under25, fill = burden_cat_under25)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 18, color = "white", linewidth = 0.4, alpha = 0.92) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 30, linetype = "dashed",
             color = "#1a1a2e", linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 50, linetype = "dashed",
             color = "#1a1a2e", linewidth = 0.8) +
  annotate("text", x = 31, y = 9.5, label = "30% threshold",
           hjust = 0, size = 3.2, color = "#1a1a2e", fontface = "italic") +
  annotate("text", x = 51, y = 9.5, label = "50% threshold",
           hjust = 0, size = 3.2, color = "#1a1a2e", fontface = "italic") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = COLORS, name = "Burden Category") +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = label_percent(scale = 1)) +
  labs(
    title   = "How Burdened Are Young Renters Across the U.S.?",
    subtitle = "Distribution of rent burden ratios for under-25 householders by state (2023)",
    x       = "Rent Burden Ratio (annual rent divided by income)",
    y       = "Number of States",
    caption = "Source: ACS 1-Year Estimates (B19049, B25064), 2023 | Excludes Puerto Rico"
  ) +
  theme_rentburden()
 
ggsave("figures/plot1_distribution.png", p1,
       width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 200, bg = "#fafafa")
print(p1)
cat("Plot 1 saved!\n")
```

```{r}
# PLOT 2: TOP 10 LEAST AFFORDABLE STATES
least_affordable <- burden_2023 %>% slice_head(n = 10)
 
p2 <- ggplot(least_affordable,
             aes(x = fct_reorder(State, burden_under25),
                 y = burden_under25,
                 fill = burden_cat_under25)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7, alpha = 0.92) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(burden_under25, 1), "%")),
            hjust = -0.15, size = 3.5, fontface = "bold",
            color = "#1a1a2e") +
  coord_flip(clip = "off") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = COLORS, name = "Burden Category") +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 58),
    labels = label_percent(scale = 1)
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = "Top 10 Least Affordable States for Young Renters",
    subtitle = "States where under-25 householders face the highest rent burden (2023)",
    x        = NULL,
    y        = "Rent Burden Ratio (%)",
    caption  = "Source: ACS 1-Year Estimates (B19049, B25064), 2023"
  ) +
  theme_rentburden() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
 
ggsave("figures/plot2_least_affordable.png", p2,
       width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 200, bg = "#fafafa")
print(p2)
cat("Plot 2 saved!\n")
```
```{r}
# PLOT 3: TOP 10 MOST AFFORDABLE STATES
most_affordable <- burden_2023 %>% slice_tail(n = 10)
 
p3 <- ggplot(most_affordable,
             aes(x = fct_reorder(State, burden_under25),
                 y = burden_under25,
                 fill = burden_cat_under25)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7, alpha = 0.92) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(burden_under25, 1), "%")),
            hjust = -0.15, size = 3.5, fontface = "bold",
            color = "#1a1a2e") +
  coord_flip(clip = "off") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = COLORS, name = "Burden Category") +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 35),
    labels = label_percent(scale = 1)
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = "Top 10 Most Affordable States for Young Renters",
    subtitle = "States where under-25 householders face the lowest rent burden (2023)",
    x        = NULL,
    y        = "Rent Burden Ratio (%)",
    caption  = "Source: ACS 1-Year Estimates (B19049, B25064), 2023"
  ) +
  theme_rentburden() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
 
ggsave("figures/plot3_most_affordable.png", p3,
       width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 200, bg = "#fafafa")
print(p3)
cat("Plot 3 saved!\n")
```

```{r}
# PLOT 4: GENERATIONAL GAP COMPARISON
burden_long <- burden_2023 %>%
  select(State, burden_under25, burden_25_44) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = c(burden_under25, burden_25_44),
    names_to  = "age_group",
    values_to = "burden_ratio"
  ) %>%
  mutate(age_group = recode(age_group,
    "burden_under25" = "Under 25 (college-age)",
    "burden_25_44"   = "Ages 25-44 (established adults)"
  ))
 
p4 <- ggplot(burden_long,
             aes(x = burden_ratio, fill = age_group)) +
  geom_histogram(
    bins      = 20,
    color     = "white",
    linewidth = 0.3,
    alpha     = 0.80,
    position  = "identity"
  ) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 30, linetype = "dashed",
             color = "#1a1a2e", linewidth = 0.9) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 50, linetype = "dashed",
             color = "#c0392b", linewidth = 0.9) +
  annotate("rect", xmin = 30, xmax = 50, ymin = 0, ymax = Inf,
           alpha = 0.04, fill = "#e67e22") +
  annotate("rect", xmin = 50, xmax = Inf, ymin = 0, ymax = Inf,
           alpha = 0.06, fill = "#c0392b") +
  annotate("text", x = 31, y = 14, label = "Burdened zone",
           hjust = 0, size = 3, color = "#e67e22", fontface = "italic") +
  annotate("text", x = 51, y = 14, label = "Severely\nburdened",
           hjust = 0, size = 3, color = "#c0392b", fontface = "italic") +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Under 25 (college-age)"          = "#e74c3c",
      "Ages 25-44 (established adults)" = "#3498db"
    ),
    name = "Age Group"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = label_percent(scale = 1)) +
  labs(
    title    = "The Generational Housing Gap",
    subtitle = "Young renters face dramatically higher rent burden than established adults (2023)",
    x        = "Rent Burden Ratio (annual rent divided by income)",
    y        = "Number of States",
    caption  = "Source: ACS 1-Year Estimates (B19049, B25064), 2023 | 30% = standard affordability threshold"
  ) +
  theme_rentburden()
 
ggsave("figures/plot4_generational_gap.png", p4,
       width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 200, bg = "#fafafa")
print(p4)
cat("Plot 4 saved!\n")
```
```{r}
# PLOT 5: ALL STATES RANKED
p5 <- ggplot(burden_2023,
             aes(x = fct_reorder(State, burden_under25),
                 y = burden_under25,
                 fill = burden_cat_under25)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.75, alpha = 0.90) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 30, linetype = "dashed",
             color = "#1a1a2e", linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 50, linetype = "dashed",
             color = "#c0392b", linewidth = 0.7) +
  annotate("text", x = 3, y = 31, label = "30% threshold",
           hjust = 0, size = 3, color = "#1a1a2e", fontface = "italic") +
  annotate("text", x = 3, y = 51, label = "50% threshold",
           hjust = 0, size = 3, color = "#c0392b", fontface = "italic") +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = COLORS, name = "Burden Category") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = label_percent(scale = 1)) +
  labs(
    title    = "Rent Burden for Under-25 Renters: All 50 States + D.C.",
    subtitle = "Ranked from most to least affordable (2023)",
    x        = NULL,
    y        = "Rent Burden Ratio (%)",
    caption  = "Source: ACS 1-Year Estimates (B19049, B25064), 2023"
  ) +
  theme_rentburden() +
  theme(
    axis.text.y     = element_text(size = 9, color = "#333355"),
    legend.position = "top",
    plot.margin     = margin(16, 30, 12, 16)
  )
 
# Must save tall so all 51 state labels fit cleanly
ggsave("figures/plot5_all_states.png", p5,
       width = 9, height = 16, dpi = 200, bg = "#fafafa")
print(p5)
cat("Plot 5 saved!\n")
```

```{r}
# PLOT 6: INCOME VS RENT SCATTERPLOT
p6 <- ggplot(burden_2023,
             aes(x = income_under25,
                 y = median_rent_annual,
                 color = burden_cat_under25,
                 label = State)) +
  geom_abline(slope = 0.30, intercept = 0,
              linetype = "dashed", color = "#27ae60", linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_abline(slope = 0.50, intercept = 0,
              linetype = "dashed", color = "#c0392b", linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.85) +
  geom_text_repel(
    data = burden_2023 %>%
      filter(burden_under25 > 40 | burden_under25 < 26),
    size          = 2.8,
    color         = "#333355",
    max.overlaps  = 15,
    segment.color = "#ccccdd"
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = COLORS, name = "Burden Category") +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = label_dollar(scale = 1)) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = label_dollar(scale = 1)) +
  annotate("text", x = 58000, y = 58000 * 0.30 + 500,
           label = "30% burden line", size = 3,
           color = "#27ae60", angle = 20, fontface = "italic") +
  annotate("text", x = 43000, y = 43000 * 0.50 + 500,
           label = "50% burden line", size = 3,
           color = "#c0392b", angle = 30, fontface = "italic") +
  labs(
    title    = "Income vs. Rent: What Is Driving Burden for Young Renters?",
    subtitle = "Each dot = one state. Points above the dashed lines are cost-burdened.",
    x        = "Median Annual Income (Under-25 Householders)",
    y        = "Median Annual Rent",
    caption  = "Source: ACS 1-Year Estimates (B19049, B25064), 2023"
  ) +
  theme_rentburden()
 
ggsave("figures/plot6_scatter.png", p6,
       width = 10, height = 7, dpi = 200, bg = "#fafafa")
print(p6)
cat("Plot 6 saved!\n")
```
```{r}
# FINAL SUMMARY TABLE
cat("\n========================================\n")
cat("   FULL RANKED TABLE: ALL STATES (2023)\n")
cat("========================================\n")
 
burden_2023 %>%
  select(
    State,
    `Income (Under 25)`  = income_under25,
    `Annual Rent`        = median_rent_annual,
    `Burden Ratio (%)`   = burden_under25,
    `Category`           = burden_cat_under25
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    `Income (Under 25)` = dollar(`Income (Under 25)`),
    `Annual Rent`       = dollar(`Annual Rent`),
    `Burden Ratio (%)` = paste0(round(`Burden Ratio (%)`, 1), "%")
  ) %>%
  print(n = 51)
 
cat("\n========================================\n")
cat("EDA complete! All 6 plots saved to figures/\n")
cat("  figures/plot1_distribution.png\n")
cat("  figures/plot2_least_affordable.png\n")
cat("  figures/plot3_most_affordable.png\n")
cat("  figures/plot4_generational_gap.png\n")
cat("  figures/plot5_all_states.png\n")
cat("  figures/plot6_scatter.png\n")
cat("========================================\n")
```
